name: Baseboard
description: Wooden baseboard trim applied along the bottom course of every interior wall, exactly where the wall plane meets the finished floor. A single block-tall band of oak planks (or stripped spruce log for a more pronounced moulding) is laid at y = floor + 1 against the inside face of the perimeter, hugging the room's footprint. Functionally it shields the wall material from scuffs and kicks; visually it resolves the floor-to-wall junction with a clean horizontal line, hiding the seam between two materials and giving the room a finished, inhabited feel. An optional quarter-round course (stairs facing inward) can be stacked above the band for a richer profile. Universal across finished interiors regardless of style — modern, victorian, rustic — and tile-able along any straight wall segment.
